2006-08-01.title: Volunteer Fighters Arrive as Tensions Rise in Abkhazia
2006-08-01.abstract: Twenty volunteer fighters arrived in the breakaway region of Abkhazia Monday amid rising tensions with Georgia.

2006-08-01.title: U.S. Hands Southern Afghan Command to NATO
2006-08-01.abstract: The difficult military role NATO is taking on was underscored by a bombing on Monday that killed eight people.

2006-08-01.title: Zimbabwe: A Trillion-Dollar Idea
2006-08-01.abstract: Still struggling with 1,200 percent annual inflation, Zimbabwe’s reserve bank said it would lop three zeros off the nation’s paper currency starting today in an attempt to make sales and bookkeeping less onerous. The bank said that Zimbabweans could exchange their existing currency for new bills until Aug. 20, after which the old bills will be worthless. Zimbabweans have become used to carrying satchels of money for even mundane shopping trips, and companies have increasingly groused that their accounting software could not cope with finances measured in the trillions and even quadrillions. Even with the change, Zimbabwe has room for improvement: a loaf of bread, which now costs $1 million, will still sell for $1,000 when the new currency comes out.

2006-08-01.title: Russian Pipeline Leak Causes Prices to Rise
2006-08-01.abstract: Oil prices spiked to about $75 a barrel after several thousand gallons of crude oil leaked from a pipeline over the weekend.

2006-08-01.title: U.S. Hands Southern Afghan Command to NATO
2006-08-01.abstract: The difficult military role NATO is taking on was underscored by a bombing on Monday that killed eight people.

2006-08-02.title: Israel's Campaign in the Gaza Strip
2006-08-02.abstract: The Times's Greg Myre reports from the Gaza Strip on Israel's latest incursion into Palestinian Territory. (Producer: Michael Kamber and Emily B. Hager)

2006-08-02.title: British Security Says Terrorist Attack on Nation Is ‘Highly Likely’
2006-08-02.abstract: British security services told Britons they face a “severe” threat, meaning that a terror attack was “highly likely.”

2006-08-02.title: Hilltop Village in Lebanon Feels Stuck in the Middle
2006-08-02.abstract: In Ain Ebel, a Christian village deep in southern Lebanon, residents are stuck in the middle of a war with which they do not identify.

2006-08-02.title: Israel Expands Ground Forces Inside Lebanon
2006-08-02.abstract: Israel sent up to 7,000 troops into Lebanon, aimed at pushing Hezbollah back from the border before a cease-fire is declared.

2006-08-02.title: Sri Lanka:  Fierce Fighting Rages; Sweden Pulls Out
2006-08-02.abstract: Government jets pounded Tamil Tiger positions in the east after the rebels fired shells at a ship ferrying more than 800 troops, as a fight over an irrigation canal dragged the country closer to full-fledged war. The fighting began last week when the military sought to gain control of a waterway in rebel-held territory. The government accuses the rebels of blocking irrigation water to thousands of farmers, mostly from the Sinhalese majority. Sweden, meanwhile, said it would pull its monitors out of the country. The Tigers want the withdrawal of all monitors called for in a 2002 truce who come from countries in the European Union, which added the group to its list of banned terrorist organizations in May. Sweden’s move would bring the Nordic-supervised monitoring mission to a third of its original 57-member size. Last week, Finland and Denmark said they were withdrawing. Only Norway and Iceland, neither in the European Union, remain.

2006-08-03.title: Ukraine Leader Forced to Name Ex-Rival as Prime Minister
2006-08-03.abstract: Viktor F. Yanukovich, the Moscow-backed politician who lost the 2004 elections, demanded the job after his party won parliamentary elections in March.

2006-08-03.title: Castro’s Younger Brother Is Focus of Attention Now
2006-08-03.abstract: The transition from Fidel Castro to Raúl, first laid out 47 years ago, has begun, and no one knows what the new government will bring.

2006-08-03.title: Israel’s Long-Term Battle
2006-08-03.abstract: As Israeli troops press the ground offensive in southern Lebanon, Israel is fighting now to win the battle of perceptions.

2006-08-03.title: End of Ukrainian Deadlock Recalls Russia’s Own 1993 Crisis
2006-08-03.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Thursday.

2006-08-03.title: Civilians Lose as Fighters Slip Into Fog of War
2006-08-03.abstract: Civilian deaths illustrate the difficulty of fighting militias that fire rockets from family gardens, orchards and village streets.

2006-08-04.title: Blair Delays Vacation for Mideast Diplomacy
2006-08-04.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Blair delayed a Caribbean vacation today saying the coming days would be crucial in the diplomatic effort to secure a cease-fire in Lebanon.

2006-08-04.title: 21 Killed by Suicide Bomber in Afghan South
2006-08-04.abstract: Hours before the attack in a bazaar, four NATO soldiers were killed and 10 wounded in two separate attacks in the same area.

2006-08-04.title: Swedish Politicians Question Safety of Its Nuclear Reactors
2006-08-04.abstract: An incident last week that led to the shutdown of 4 of Sweden’s 10 nuclear reactors has thrust the issue of atomic power back onto the national agenda.

2006-08-04.title: Compromise in Ukraine May Have Been Costly
2006-08-04.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Friday.

2006-08-04.title: Muslims Warn of Surge in Terrorism
2006-08-04.abstract: Iran’s president, Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, suggested that the solution to the crisis was to eliminate Israel.

2006-08-05.title: Israeli Strikes Hit Dozens of Targets in Lebanon
2006-08-05.abstract: Meanwhile, an Israeli commando raid in Tyre turned into some of the most intense face-to-face fighting of the war so far.

2006-08-05.title: Canada: 18th Terror Cell Arrest
2006-08-05.abstract: The police arrested an 18th man in a suspected terrorist cell that was believed to be plotting to use truck bombs, take hostages and cut off the prime minister’s head. The suspect, Ibrahim Alkhalel Aboud, 19, was arrested at his home in a Toronto suburb. The other suspects, all Muslims and including five minors, were charged in June.

2006-08-05.title: U.S. and France Reach Deal on Mideast Resolution
2006-08-05.abstract: The two countries reached agreement on a Security Council resolution to halt the fighting in Lebanon and lay out plans for a permanent ceasefire.

2006-08-05.title: Argentina: Prison for ‘Dirty War’ Policeman
2006-08-05.abstract: In the first trial on charges of human rights violations since a pair of amnesty laws were overturned last year, Julio Simón, a federal police official who was in charge of a clandestine detention center during the military dictatorship that ruled the country from 1976 to 1983, was sentenced to 25 years in prison. He was accused of the kidnapping, torture and forced disappearance of a Buenos Aires couple in 1978, as well as taking illegal custody of their 8-month-old daughter. “This is a historic day that opens the path to all the trials that need to take place,” said Estela de Carlotto, director of the Grandmothers of the Plaza de Mayo, a leading human rights group.

2006-08-05.title: A One-Man Embassy Adjusts to a Smaller Portfolio
2006-08-05.abstract: The ambassador-designate of the newly independent Montenegro sends his dispatches from a laptop in his son’s bedroom.

2006-08-06.title: ‘Civil War’ Is Uttered, and White House’s Iraq Strategy Is Dealt a Blow
2006-08-06.abstract: A potential “civil war” in Iraq will make it even more difficult to convince battle-weary Americans that the war in Iraq is winnable.

2006-08-06.title: Rice Says Draft U.N. Resolution Is Only ‘First Step’
2006-08-06.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice cautioned today that she did not expect an immediate end to violence in the Middle East.

2006-08-06.title: 27 Years Later, a Formal Inquiry Begins Into Khmer Rouge Atrocities
2006-08-06.abstract: After years of wrangling between Cambodia and the U.N., formal proceedings have begun against surviving leaders of the brutal Khmer Rouge regime.

2006-08-06.title: U.S. and France Back Plan to End Lebanon Fight
2006-08-06.abstract: A Security Council draft resolution laid out a plan for a permanent cease-fire and political settlement.

2006-08-06.title: In Shiite Areas of Baghdad, 9 Found Dead
2006-08-06.abstract: Though officials did not reveal the sectarian identities of the bodies, victims of killings committed by Shiite death squads are often dumped in Shiite-dominated areas.

2006-08-07.title: G.I. Tells Why He Testified in Rape-Murder Inquiry
2006-08-07.abstract: Pfc. Justin Watt described today how he became the whistleblower in the case and how he feared for his life.

2006-08-07.title: 4 G.I.’s Tell of How Iraqi Raid Went Wrong
2006-08-07.abstract: Four American soldiers facing a military hearing have provided details of what happened in the minutes before and after three Iraqis were shot dead.

2006-08-07.title: Iran Says It Will Ignore U.N. Deadline on Uranium Program
2006-08-07.abstract: The country’s chief national security official maintained that Iran would not halt enrichment as a precondition to negotiations.

2006-08-07.title: Iraq Hearing on Rape and Murder Opens
2006-08-07.abstract: A former U.S. soldier accused of rape and murder told fellow soldiers that “all Iraqis are bad people,” according to testimony.

2006-08-07.title: Hezbollah Rockets Ravaging Israel’s Forests
2006-08-07.abstract: Officials estimated today that almost 3,000 acres of forest have been damaged by fire in the fighting.

2006-08-08.title: Surviving in Bourj al-Barajneh
2006-08-08.abstract: Archie Tse reports from Bourj al-Barajneh, a Palestinian refugee camp in Lebanon.  (Produced by: Emily B. Hager and Archie Tse)

2006-08-08.title: Chad’s Switch to Beijing’s Side Draws Angry Response in Taiwan
2006-08-08.abstract: The switch in diplomatic relations also raises the possibility that China may use its influence with Sudan and Chadian rebels.

2006-08-08.title: Hit on Bridge North of Tyre Isolates South of Lebanon
2006-08-08.abstract: The crossing point carried almost all of the traffic over the Litani River, which separates Lebanon’s north from its south.

2006-08-08.title: Dry Forests in Northern Israel Are Damaged as Hezbollah’s Rocket Attacks Ignite Fires
2006-08-08.abstract: Officials estimated that as much as 9,000 acres of land have been damaged by fire in the nearly four weeks of cross-border fighting.

2006-08-08.title: Arms Exporter Faults U.S. Sanctions
2006-08-08.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian Newspapers on Tuesday.

2006-08-09.title: Diner Beware: Turisti Pay More in Roman Restaurants
2006-08-09.abstract: The practice of charging tourists more is committed daily, even hourly, in Italy, and when executed properly, the turista are none the wiser.

2006-08-09.title: Left or Right, Israelis Are Pro-War
2006-08-09.abstract: The harder the war with Hezbollah becomes, the more the Israeli public wants it to proceed, and with greater force.

2006-08-09.title: Bank Robbery and Bombs Kill 24 in Iraq
2006-08-09.abstract: Also Tuesday, testimony continued in a hearing for four soldiers charged with rape and murder in Iraq.

2006-08-09.title: Israeli Cabinet Backs Wider Ground Raids in Lebanon
2006-08-09.abstract: After four weeks of inconclusive fighting, Israel decided today to expand its ground operation in Lebanon to push Hezbollah and its rocket launchers northward.

2006-08-09.title: Russia Sentences Ex-Officer to 13 Years for Spying
2006-08-09.abstract: The officer, identified as Col. Sergei V. Skripal, was sentenced to 13 years in prison for treason stemming from a lengthy collaboration with Britain’s secret intelligence service.

2006-08-10.title: Four Arrested in U.S. Journalist’s Kidnapping
2006-08-10.abstract: Marines searched four houses where military officials now believe the journalist Jill Carroll was held captive.

2006-08-10.title: After Congo Vote, Most Are Hoping for a Round 2
2006-08-10.abstract: Most Congolese want a second round of voting, and many think that for stability’s sake it would be better if the president is chosen in a runoff.

2006-08-10.title: White Lace and Rocket Fire: Israeli Couples Won’t Let the War Ruin Their Wedding Day
2006-08-10.abstract: Fifteen couples from northern Israel whose weddings had been canceled because of the war married in a group ceremony Tuesday in Tel Aviv, Israel.

2006-08-10.title: Dry Run Was Planned in Bomb Plot, Officials Say
2006-08-10.abstract: A top homeland security official said suspects rounded up in Britain began investigating nonstop flights to the U.S. in recent days.

2006-08-10.title: Big Hurdles Remain in Detecting Liquid Explosives
2006-08-10.abstract: Security agencies have made little progress in deploying technology that could help defend against liquid explosives.

2006-08-11.title: Shelling in Northern Israel
2006-08-11.abstract: Richard A. Oppel Jr. reports on an Arab Israeli family under siege from Hezbollah attacks in northern Israel. (Produced by Michael Kamber)

2006-08-11.title: France: Amnesty Deadline for Immigrants
2006-08-11.abstract: Thousands of illegal immigrants with children in French schools hastened to meet an amnesty deadline today to apply for residency papers. The Interior Ministry said that it had received about 24,000 applications and that it expected to deliver as many as 6,000 residency permits. The campaign to help illegal immigrant families with school-age children came under pressure from rights organizations, which asked the government to grant a blanket amnesty to families, rather than dealing on a case-by-case basis. Families are eligible for amnesty if at least one child was born in France or arrived before the age of 13 and has been in school in France for two years.

2006-08-11.title: Terror Plot Foiled; Airports Quickly Clamp Down
2006-08-11.abstract: British officials arrested 24 men, but said they believed that some plotters were probably still at large, requiring increased airport security.

2006-08-11.title: Israel Holds Off on Drive to the North
2006-08-11.abstract: Israel warned residents of southern Beirut to evacuate even as it held off expanding its military operation.

2006-08-11.title: Resolution to Halt Fighting in Lebanon Is Unanimously Approved
2006-08-11.abstract: The resolution also calls for the deployment of Lebanese and U.N. forces in southern Lebanon, and the withdrawal "in parallel" by Israel.

2006-08-12.title: No Longer Embarrassed, Finland Embraces Its Monster Band
2006-08-12.abstract: The demonic quintet Lordi has been transformed from national scourges into national heroes after winning the Eurovision contest.

2006-08-12.title: Khmer Rouge’s Last Bastion Calls on Spirits for Luck
2006-08-12.abstract: In a former Khmer Rouge stronghold, Pol Pot and other past leaders are remembered with loyalty and affection.

2006-08-12.title: Protest Erupts as World Bank Official Gets a Ticket in Kenya
2006-08-12.abstract: The official said he had been shaken down for a bribe during a traffic stop, but Kenyan authorities said nothing improper happened.

2006-08-12.title: Pakistan’s Help in Averting a Terror Attack Is a Double-Edged Sword
2006-08-12.abstract: This week’s case is a tacit acknowledgment of what a nexus Pakistan remains for would-be terrorists from halfway across the world.

2006-08-12.title: An Audit Sharply Criticizes Iraq’s Bookkeeping
2006-08-12.abstract: The audit portrays Iraq as a country where elementary accounting errors of up to a billion dollars are routine.

2006-08-13.title: African Grandmothers Rally for AIDS Orphans
2006-08-13.abstract: A gathering in Toronto is believed to be the first dedicated to helping grandmothers cope with AIDS.

2006-08-13.title: Accounts After 2005 London Bombings Point to Al Qaeda Role From Pakistan
2006-08-13.abstract: If suspects in last week’s foiled plot met with members of Al Qaeda in Pakistan, it may not be the first time such a meeting took place.

2006-08-13.title: British Muslims Criticize Blair and Policies; Police Broaden Search for Evidence
2006-08-13.abstract: Muslim leaders said the prime minister’s policies in Iraq and the Mideast provided “ammunition to extremists who threaten us all.”

2006-08-13.title: After U.N. Accord, Israel Expands Push in Lebanon
2006-08-13.abstract: As Israel poured troops into Lebanon in its deepest push yet, the U.N. secretary general announced that a cease-fire would take effect on Monday morning.

2006-08-13.title: Terrorism Experts Say Focus on Al Qaeda Misses a Broader Threat
2006-08-13.abstract: Experts said the focus of officials and the public on Al Qaeda, a term with deep connotations but elusive meaning, may be misplaced.

2006-08-14.title: In a Political Move, Lebanon Offers an Army That All of Its Sects Can Accept: Its Own
2006-08-14.abstract: Prime Minister Fouad Siniora’s offer to send the Lebanese Army into the Hezbollah-dominated south proved central to breaking the diplomatic impasse over Israel’s invasion.

2006-08-14.title: Mexican Leftist Remains Defiant as  Recount Ends
2006-08-14.abstract: Manuel López Obrador vowed to continue a campaign of civil disobedience to protest what he sees as a fraudulent election.

2006-08-14.title: A Venezuelan Comedian Hopes to Unseat Chávez
2006-08-14.abstract: Benjamín Rausseo, Venezuela’s best-known stand-up comedian, is now a contender in presidential elections.

2006-08-14.title: Novelist’s Revelation Causes Outrage in Germany
2006-08-14.abstract: Günter Grass’s confession over the weekend that he served in the Waffen SS, the combat arm of the Nazi organization that carried out the Holocaust, has produced dismay in its purest form.

2006-08-14.title: Speaker of Iraq’s Parliament Considers Quitting
2006-08-14.abstract: The speaker said Shiite and Kurdish legislators have banded together to try to push him out.

2006-08-15.title: Beirut, After the Cease-Fire
2006-08-15.abstract: The Times's Archie Tse reports from a park in central Beirut where refugees began departing for their homes on Monday.

2006-08-15.title: The U.N. Puts a Rush Order for a Force of 15,000
2006-08-15.abstract: The United Nations took up the task on Monday of creating in record time a strong force to patrol southern Lebanon.

2006-08-15.title: Koizumi Further Deepens Debate Over Shrine
2006-08-15.abstract: Prime Minister Junichiro Koizumi of Japan prayed at the Yasukuni Shrine despite warnings from Asian neighbors.

2006-08-15.title: Returning Home to Ruins: Shock Is Mixed With Outrage
2006-08-15.abstract: Thousands streamed back on Monday into the ruined streets of Beirut. Some swore revenge; others simply wept.

2006-08-15.title: Speaker of Iraqi Parliament May Step Down
2006-08-15.abstract: The speaker said Shiite and Kurdish legislators have banded together to try to push him out.

2006-08-16.title: Israel, After the Cease-Fire
2006-08-16.abstract: Greg Myre reports from Northern Israel on the aftermath of the cease-fire. (Producers: Michael Kamber and Jigar Mehta)

2006-08-16.title: Georgia: Call for More Reserve Troops
2006-08-16.abstract: President Mikheil Saakashvili called for his government to double the size of its military reserve force, to 100,000 soldiers. He also called for more reservist camps to be established and built to Western standards. The announcements came as tensions have risen between Georgia and two breakaway regions within its borders — Abkhazia and South Ossetia — and there have been claims by Abkhaz leaders that Georgia is preparing for war. Mr. Saakashvili has repeatedly denied that and has criticized Russia for giving the regions open support. His troops have not been idle. Last month, Georgian security forces swept through the Kodori Gorge, a mountainous area in the Caucasus near the Russian border, and chased away a paramilitary force whose leader said he would no longer recognize Georgian rule.

2006-08-16.title: Grass’s Publisher Releases His Memoir Early
2006-08-16.abstract: Steidl Verlag is taking advantage of the ferocious controversy around Günter Grass’s admission that he was briefly a member of the Waffen SS.

2006-08-16.title: A Cruel Sequel: Bombs Destroy Kids' Memorial
2006-08-16.abstract: An attack shredded a memorial commemorating two dozen children who were killed last year by a car bomber.

2006-08-16.title: Iran Says It Is Open to Enrichment Halt
2006-08-16.abstract: The foreign minister also said that Iran was still planning to give a formal response to a U.N. proposal by Aug. 22.

2006-08-17.title: Europe Says It Will Unify Effort in Fight on Terrorism
2006-08-17.abstract: New measures could include blocking Web sites and fingerprinting or iris-scanning airline passengers.

2006-08-17.title: Doctor Suspended After Boston Rape Is Revealed
2006-08-17.abstract: A surgeon has been suspended from practicing in Queensland State, after the authorities there discovered that he had been convicted in 1981 of raping a nurse in Massachusetts. The doctor, Eugene Sherry, a New Zealander who was 27 and a resident at Brigham and Women’s Hospital in Boston at the time of the charges, was convicted with two other doctors in a trial that riveted Boston. After six months in prison, Dr. Sherry moved to Australia in 1983, and his conviction went undetected until this week. His case brought comparisons to the case last year of Jayant Patel, an American-trained doctor charged by Queensland authorities with the deaths of several patients. Dr. Sherry’s lawyer told the newspaper The Australian that his client had been wrongfully convicted in 1982.

2006-08-17.title: Alfredo Stroessner, 93, Paraguayan Ex-Dictator, Dies
2006-08-17.abstract: General Stroessner, South America’s most enduring dictator during the cold war, died in exile.

2006-08-17.title: South Africa: Aristide Welcome to Stay
2006-08-17.abstract: The deposed Haitian president, Jean-Bertrand Aristide, is welcome to remain in exile in South Africa for as long as he likes, but neither he nor the South African government anticipates that he will stay forever, the foreign minister, Nkosazana Dlamini-Zuma, told Parliament. Mr. Aristide, left, who left Haiti in 2004, lives outside Pretoria in circumstances akin to those of a cabinet minister, she said. Critics have accused President Thabo Mbeki of according luxury accommodations to Mr. Aristide, who was widely accused of corruption and misrule. Ms. Dlamini-Zuma said, however, that Mr. Aristide’s exile fit an international mandate to create peace and stability in Haiti.

2006-08-17.title: Gas is Focus of Meeting Between Russian and Ukrainian Leaders
2006-08-17.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Thursday.

2006-08-18.title: French Candidate’s Rifled Home Ruffles Her Political Feathers
2006-08-18.abstract: PARIS, Aug. 18 — It could be the kind of story that blossoms into a major scandal or it could soon be forgotten as yet another skirmish between the leading contenders to succeed President Jacques Chirac next year. Still, as Parisians begin returning from vacation to a damp and grey city, it may provide some distraction.

2006-08-18.title: Marines May Have Excised Evidence on 24 Iraqi Deaths
2006-08-18.abstract: An investigation into the killings of 24 Iraqis in Haditha found that the U.S. marines involved appear to have destroyed or withheld evidence.

2006-08-18.title: U.S. Predicts Fast Action at U.N. if Sanctions on Iran Are Needed
2006-08-18.abstract: A senior Bush administration official said punishment “will be well deserved” if Iran refused to halt uranium enrichment.

2006-08-18.title: 2 Avian Flu Cases Confirmed in Indonesia
2006-08-18.abstract: One patient died from a confirmed case. Indonesia has now had more deaths, a total of 45, from the disease than any other country.

2006-08-18.title: Chinese Take a Turn at Turning a Sub-Saharan Profit
2006-08-18.abstract: China seems to be offering Africa something new, a straightforward business relationship between equals based on mutual interest.

2006-08-19.title: Israel: Justice Minister to Quit Over Kiss Charges
2006-08-19.abstract: Justice Minister Haim Ramon said he would resign and face charges of sexual harassment lodged by a female soldier who had said Mr. Ramon forcibly kissed her in a government office last month. The announcement came a day after Menachem Mazuz, the attorney general, said he planned to indict Mr. Ramon, who is considered a close ally of Prime Minister Ehud Olmert. The incident took place on July 12, the day Hezbollah staged a cross-border raid, igniting the fighting with Israel. “I am sure that I will succeed in court,” Mr. Ramon said in a statement. “A kiss of two, three seconds, based on the version of the complainant, cannot be turned into a criminal act.”.

2006-08-19.title: Villagers Cheer as Lebanese Army Marches South
2006-08-19.abstract: The Lebanese Army reached the country’s southern border with Israel for the first time in decades.

2006-08-19.title: Baghdad Shut to Cars on Eve of Pilgrimage So Dire in ’05
2006-08-19.abstract: The last-minute ban is part of what appears to be a concerted effort to avoid the sectarian-inspired panic during last year’s pilgrimage.

2006-08-19.title: Colombia’s Coca Survives U.S. Plan to Uproot It
2006-08-19.abstract: A $4.7 billion effort to slash Colombia’s coca crop has left the availability of cocaine on U.S. streets unchanged.

2006-08-19.title: With ‘Minefields’ at Home, War Isn’t Over for Lebanese
2006-08-19.abstract: Thousands of bomblets released by shells fired into southern Lebanon litter everything from streets to farmland.

2006-08-20.title: Cannabis Cafes Get Nudge to Fringes of a Dutch City
2006-08-20.abstract: Fed up with drug tourists, the mayor of Maastricht wants to move the city’s marijuana cafes to its outskirts.

2006-08-20.title: Venezuela Says It Seized 4 Spies; U.S. Embassy Denies Knowledge
2006-08-20.abstract: President Hugo Chávez taunted the Bush administration for making Venezuela a target of high-level intelligence scrutiny.

2006-08-20.title: Lydia, Duchess of Bedford, 88, Pioneer in Noble-Tourism, Dies
2006-08-20.abstract: The Duchess of Bedford helped transform Woburn Abbey in Bedfordshire, England, into a pioneering and lucrative example of blueblood tourism.

2006-08-20.title: Iran Reiterates It Won’t End Nuclear Program
2006-08-20.abstract: Iran also fired 10 short-range missiles today amid growing international pressure over its nuclear program.

2006-08-20.title: For Families of Captives, a Long Wait in the Dark
2006-08-20.abstract: The families of the two Israeli army reservists captured in a Hezbollah raid that sparked the latest war still await their fate.

2006-08-21.title: As 2nd Hussein Trial Starts, Kurds Are Fixated
2006-08-21.abstract: Facing a charge of genocide for trying to annihilate Iraq’s Kurds, Saddam Hussein refused to enter a plea.

2006-08-21.title: Iran’s Leader Vows to Continue Nuclear Program
2006-08-21.abstract: The remarks came a day before the nation was expected to respond to a proposal for Iran to stop enriching uranium.

2006-08-21.title: Bush Urges Swift Deployment of Lebanon Force
2006-08-21.abstract: President Bush also said the U.S. would contribute $230 million in humanitarian and reconstruction aid to Lebanon.

2006-08-21.title: Iran Fires Practice Missiles and Affirms Nuclear Stance
2006-08-21.abstract: Iran fired 10 short-range missiles amid growing international pressure over its nuclear program.

2006-08-21.title: South Korea Reveals Arrest of Spy from the North
2006-08-21.abstract: The man is accused of spying on potential military targets, including nuclear power plants and United States military bases in South Korea.

2006-08-22.title: New Pakistani Terror Plot Foiled, Indian Police Say
2006-08-22.abstract: The Indian police said today that they had shot and killed a Pakistani terrorist on Monday night.

2006-08-22.title: Germans Eye Network Behind Train Bomb Suspects
2006-08-22.abstract: A top German security official said today that two Lebanese men suspected of planting bombs on two German trains might have had support from a network of Islamic extremists here and in Lebanon.

2006-08-22.title: Prosecutors Detail Atrocities in Hussein’s Trial
2006-08-22.abstract: Saddam Hussein sat stone-faced as prosecutors described how he and six co-defendants embarked on a campaign to eliminate Kurds in northern Iraq.

2006-08-22.title: Russian Plane Crashes, Killing All 169 Aboard
2006-08-22.abstract: It was the third major aviation disaster in or near Russia in less than four months.

2006-08-22.title: Israeli Official Is Under Investigation
2006-08-22.abstract: The investigation of President Moshe Katsav comes at a time of multiple controversies for the Israeli government.

2006-08-23.abstract: On Aug. 2, Israeli commandos in Baalbek raided a house belonging to Hassan Nasrallah the plasterer rather than Hassan Nasrallah the Hezbollah leader.

2006-08-23.title: Plane Crash Adds to Russia’s August Miseries
2006-08-23.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Wednesday.

2006-08-23.title: Iran Won’t Give Promise to End Uranium Effort
2006-08-23.abstract: Iran said it was prepared to enter “serious talks” over its nuclear program, but did not agree to suspend uranium enrichment.

2006-08-23.title: Ukraine Leader Promises Not to Siphon Russian Gas
2006-08-23.abstract: The assurance was the latest step by Ukraine’s new prime minister, Viktor F. Yanukovich, to unwind some of the conflicts between Ukraine and Russia.

2006-08-23.title: Iran Sanctions Could Fracture Coalition
2006-08-23.abstract: Now that carrots have failed to convince Iran to suspend uranium enrichment, can the U.S. convince coalition members to take out their sticks?

2006-08-24.title: 2 Ekaterinburg Parties May be Ousted from Elections
2006-08-24.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Thursday.

2006-08-24.title: Human Rights Group Accuses Israel of War Crimes
2006-08-24.abstract: Amnesty International said Israel’s bombing campaign amounted to indiscriminate attacks on Lebanese civilians.

2006-08-24.title: India: World Bank Loosens Purse Strings
2006-08-24.abstract: The World Bank has approved $672 million in assistance to help India improve health care for women and children and to combat tuberculosis.

2006-08-24.title: Ukraine’s Premier Pledges to Stop Taking Gas From Russian Pipes
2006-08-24.abstract: The pledge was the latest indication that Viktor F. Yanukovich, Ukraine’s new prime minister, aims to improve relations with Russia.

2006-08-24.title: France Pledges to Send More Troops to Lebanon
2006-08-24.abstract: The number of French soldiers on the ground in Lebanon would rise to 2,000, President Jacques Chirac said today.

2006-08-25.title: Chinese Peasants’ Advocate Sentenced to 51 Months in Jail
2006-08-25.abstract: The advocate, Chen Guangcheng, earned the enmity of local Communist Party leaders when he sought to organize a lawsuit against forced abortions and sterilizations.

2006-08-25.title: Lebanese and Aid Groups Find Dangers in the Rubble
2006-08-25.abstract: Aid agencies say that a third of the roughly 900,000 Lebanese refugees cannot return to their homes because of unexploded cluster bombs.

2006-08-25.title: Russia Says Iran Sanctions Are Premature
2006-08-25.abstract: Russia’s opposition left in doubt U.S. efforts to intensify pressure on Iran over its nuclear programs.

2006-08-25.title: 8 Killed in Raid by U.S. Forces in Afghanistan
2006-08-25.abstract: United States forces acknowledged killing a child and injuring a woman but said the seven men also killed were “Qaeda facilitators” who had opened fire on them.

2006-08-25.title: Progress Reported in Efforts to Free Journalists
2006-08-25.abstract: The Hamas-run Palestinian Authority says it is trying to secure the release of two kidnapped Western journalists.

2006-08-26.title: Jets Grounded in U.S. and Ireland; Alert Raised at Houston Airport
2006-08-26.abstract: An anonymous caller told the police that a liquid explosive had been planted on board a flight from New York to Ireland.

2006-08-26.title: Ukraine: 9 Years in U.S. Prison for Ex-Premier
2006-08-26.abstract: A federal judge in San Francisco sentenced Pavlo Lazarenko, a former prime minister of Ukraine accused of stealing $114 million while in office from 1996 to 1997, to nine years in an American prison for money laundering, wire fraud and transporting stolen goods. He was also fined $10 million. He fled to the United States in 1999, seeking asylum, but instead was arrested and charged with siphoning tens of millions of dollars of public money into his personal accounts and attempting to hide some $21 million in American banks, mostly in San Francisco. Mr. Lazarenko, 53, who was convicted in 2004 and has since been under house arrest, is the first former head of government tried in the United States since the Panamanian dictator Manuel Antonio Noriega, who was convicted of money laundering and drug trafficking by a federal jury in Miami in 1992 and sentenced to 40 years.

2006-08-26.title: After 9 Months Adrift at Sea, Survivors Tell Their Stories
2006-08-26.abstract: Some greeted the Mexican fishermen as folk heroes, but others cast a skeptical eye on their epic story of seamanship and survival.

2006-08-26.title: East Timor: U.N. Peacekeepers to Return
2006-08-26.abstract: The Security Council unanimously adopted a Japanese-drafted resolution setting up a new United Nations peacekeeping mission for East Timor, the Pacific nation that became independent in 2002 but suffered widespread unrest this spring. The mission, which would have to be renewed in six months, includes 1,600 police officers and 34 military liaison officers but no major military component. It is charged with maintaining order as the country prepares for its first elections in 2007.

2006-08-26.title: Europe Pledges a Larger Force Inside Lebanon
2006-08-26.abstract: Officials cautioned that the peacekeeping force would not be used to disarm Hezbollah.

2006-08-27.title: Missing Chechen Was Secret Bride of Terror Leader
2006-08-27.abstract: A woman’s disappearance offers a glimpse into the life of Russia’s most wanted man, who died in an explosion.

2006-08-27.title: 52 Killed in Iraq; Bomber Attacks Newspaper
2006-08-27.abstract: In Baghdad, a suicide car bomber attacked Iraq’s largest newspaper and a bomb exploded inside a commuter bus.

2006-08-27.title: Turks Knock on Europe’s Door With Evidence That Islam and Capitalism Can Coexist
2006-08-27.abstract: As Turkey seeks to join the European Union, the Anatolia region has been able to blend Muslim religious fervor, hard work and marketplace values.

2006-08-27.title: U.S. and Venezuela at Odds, and Seized Cargo Is Just the Half of It
2006-08-27.abstract: Venezuela seized diplomatic baggage amid growing distaste in its government for the United States’ moves to step up spying operations.

2006-08-27.title: Disputes Spur His Critics, Karzai Says
2006-08-27.abstract: Afghanistan’s president said that recent criticism of his leadership stemmed from disagreements that he had had with some partners of the United States-led coalition.

2006-08-28.title: From Hamas Figure, an Unusual Self-Criticism
2006-08-28.abstract: A well-known Hamas official said that much of the blame for the decline of Gaza belonged to Palestinians.

2006-08-28.title: Tribal Leader’s Killing Incites Riots in Pakistan
2006-08-28.abstract: Violent protests broke out after the killing of a prominent rebel tribal leader in a fierce battle with the army.

2006-08-28.title: Series of Blasts Kill 3 in Turkish Resorts
2006-08-28.abstract: A hardline Kurdish separatist group claimed responsibility for attacks on Sunday.

2006-08-28.title: Shiite Militia Clashes With Iraqi Troops
2006-08-28.abstract: At least 28 gunmen and civilians were killed when the Iraqi army clashed today with members of a militia loyal to Moktada al-Sadr, Iraqi officials said.

2006-08-28.title: An Ex-Official Offers Glimpse of Iranian Views of U.S.
2006-08-28.abstract: Some Iranian leaders see Iran’s conflict with the United States over its nuclear program as the country’s effort to never again allow Washington to have an upper hand.

2006-08-29.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 17 Afghans; Attacks Persist in the South
2006-08-29.abstract: A Taliban spokesman claimed responsibility for the explosion, which was the second deadliest suicide bombing in southern Afghanistan this year.

2006-08-29.title: 9th Person Charged in Plot to Bomb Airliners
2006-08-29.abstract: Nabeel Hussain was accused of conspiracy to murder and planning to smuggle “improvised explosive devices onto aircraft and assemble and detonate them on board.”

2006-08-29.title: Sudan Shuns U.S. Plea for U.N. Force in Darfur
2006-08-29.abstract: Sudan’s president has said that U.N. troops would be seen as occupying forces and that Sudan’s military would fight them.

2006-08-29.title: Pipeline Blast Kills 67 After Clashes at Iraqi City
2006-08-29.abstract: At least 67 people were killed when looters who were stealing from an oil pipeline inadvertently set off a huge explosion outside Diwaniya.

2006-08-29.title: Islamic Revival in Syria Is Led by Women
2006-08-29.abstract: The growth of Koranic schools for girls, rare in the Arab world, is a sign that Syria is becoming religious.

2006-08-30.title: Revival of Brutality in Chechnya
2006-08-30.abstract: Men serving as the police tortured a woman accused of adultery. The Times's C.J. Chivers reports on the revival of brutality among Chechnya's pro-Kremlin forces. (Producer: Adam B. Ellick)

2006-08-30.title: Second Party in Russia Met with Some Skepticism
2006-08-30.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Wednesday.

2006-08-30.title: Mexico: Mediation, but City Closes
2006-08-30.abstract: Striking teachers and state officials met with federal mediators in Mexico City to try to end the civil unrest that has gripped Oaxaca for three months.

2006-08-30.title: Iran: U.S. Grants Visa to Ex-Leader
2006-08-30.abstract: The State Department issued a visa to Iran’s former president, Mohammad Khatami, allowing him to make a private visit that will include speeches in Chicago and Washington and a conference at the United Nations.

2006-08-30.title: Iranian President Meets Press and Is Challenged
2006-08-30.abstract: Reporters questioned the government’s economic program and its tolerance of a critical press.

2006-08-31.title: Israeli Police Capture Palestinian at Embassy
2006-08-31.abstract: A Palestinian man  today scaled a wall into the parking lot of the British Embassy and demanded asylum.

2006-08-31.title: Iran Is Defiant as U.S. Drafts Sanctions
2006-08-31.abstract: Iran ignored a deadline set by the U.N. to suspend enrichment of uranium or face the threat sanctions.

2006-08-31.title: Marine Accused of Murder in Iraq Will Not Face the Death Penalty
2006-08-31.abstract: Pfc. John J. Jodka III is charged along with six other marines and a Navy corpsman in the death of an Iraqi civilian in Hamdania, a Sunni stronghold west of Baghdad.

2006-08-31.title: Iran: Writer Freed
2006-08-31.abstract: Ramin Jahanbegloo, one of Iran’s best-known scholars, was released after four months in prison on suspicion of working with the United States to overthrow the Iranian government, local news media reported.

2006-08-31.title: U.N. Threatens to Halt its Aid Efforts in Sri Lanka
2006-08-31.abstract: The Sri Lankan government angrily denied accusations by U.N. cease-fire monitors that it had executed aid workers.
